target.title: [AI] fix(daemon): set default NODE_OPTIONS --max-old-space-size in gateway service env
target.body:
## Summary

Managed gateway and node services now receive a default `--max-old-space-size=8192` heap flag via generated systemd/launchd/schtasks service files, with an optional `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` env override. Ambient `NODE_OPTIONS` from the host process are never persisted into managed service environments.

- Problem: Gateway crash-loops with default Node heap (~4 GB) under sustained load. `openclaw daemon install` did not set any heap configuration in generated service files.
- Solution: Generate only the OpenClaw-owned `--max-old-space-size` flag in managed service environments. Support `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` override. Explicitly drop all ambient `NODE_OPTIONS` from the host process.
- What changed: `src/daemon/service-env.ts` — `resolveServiceNodeOptions()` generates only the validated heap flag; `buildCommonServiceEnvironment()` applies it as `NODE_OPTIONS`
- What did NOT change: Proxy env, CA certs, PATH, HOME, and all other service environment fields; `daemon install` workflow

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #96203
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Gateway crash-loops with default Node heap (~4 GB) under sustained load. The `openclaw daemon install` path did not configure any heap limit for managed services. This PR adds a default `--max-old-space-size=8192` (8 GB) for gateway and node daemon services, configurable via `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB`.

### Security: Ambient NODE_OPTIONS dropped

The original implementation read `env.NODE_OPTIONS` from the host process and preserved or appended to it. This could persist hostile flags (`--require`, `--inspect`, `--expose-gc`) into managed services, re-opening a startup preload boundary. The fix generates only the OpenClaw-owned `--max-old-space-size` flag and never reads host `NODE_OPTIONS`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: Managed gateway/node service environments receive only the OpenClaw-owned `--max-old-space-size` heap flag. Generated systemd unit files contain `Environment=NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`. Hostile ambient flags are dropped.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v24.13.1, commit `3ab0222`, OpenClaw v2026.6.9

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Full test suite (97 tests)
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/service-env.test.ts --run

# Generated systemd unit proof (no daemon install needed)
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '
import { buildServiceEnvironment, buildNodeServiceEnvironment } from "./src/daemon/service-env.ts";
import { buildSystemdUnit } from "./src/daemon/systemd-unit.ts";
// Gateway default, heap override, node service, hostile NODE_OPTIONS, reinstall
'
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/service-env.test.ts --run

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  97 passed (97)
   Duration  1.19s
```

Generated systemd unit file proof (equivalent to `openclaw daemon install` output):

```console
=== 1. Generated systemd gateway unit (default) ===
  Description=OpenClaw Gateway
  ExecStart=/usr/bin/node --import tsx /opt/openclaw/src/gateway/server.ts
  Environment=NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192
  Environment=OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER=openclaw
  Environment=OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND=gateway
  Environment=OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION=2026.6.9

=== 2. Generated systemd gateway unit (with OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=6144) ===
  Environment=NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=6144

=== 3. Generated systemd node unit (default) ===
  Environment=NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192
  Environment=OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER=openclaw
  Environment=OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND=node
  Environment=OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION=2026.6.9

=== 4. Ambient NODE_OPTIONS security proof ===
Input NODE_OPTIONS:  --require /tmp/evil.js --inspect=0.0.0.0:9229
Output NODE_OPTIONS: --max-old-space-size=8192
--require dropped:  true
--inspect dropped:  true

=== 5. Reinstall/upgrade scenario ===
NODE_OPTIONS after reinstall: --max-old-space-size=8192
```

Behavior matrix:

| Scenario | Generated `NODE_OPTIONS` | Hostile flags |
|----------|--------------------------|:--:|
| Default gateway install | `--max-old-space-size=8192` | — |
| `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=6144` | `--max-old-space-size=6144` | — |
| `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=0` | `undefined` (disabled) | — |
| `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=invalid` | `--max-old-space-size=8192` (fallback) | — |
| Node service install | `--max-old-space-size=8192` | — |
| Host with `--require evil.js` | `--max-old-space-size=8192` | ✅ dropped |
| Host with `--inspect=0.0.0.0:9229` | `--max-old-space-size=8192` | ✅ dropped |
| Reinstall/upgrade | `--max-old-space-size=8192` | ✅ clean |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. Generated systemd unit files contain `Environment=NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`
  2. `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` correctly overrides the heap size in generated service files
  3. `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=0` correctly disables the heap flag
  4. Hostile ambient flags (`--require`, `--inspect`) are completely dropped
  5. Both gateway and node services receive the heap configuration
  6. Reinstall/upgrade regenerates clean NODE_OPTIONS
  7. All 97 tests pass

- **What was not tested**: Actual `openclaw daemon install` with `systemctl` on a real Linux host (requires root). The generated systemd unit content is identical — `daemon install` calls `buildSystemdUnit(buildServiceEnvironment(...))` internally.

## Root Cause

The original `resolveServiceNodeOptions()` read `env.NODE_OPTIONS` from the host process and appended `--max-old-space-size` to preserve existing flags. This was intended to be non-destructive but created a security boundary violation: any flag in the host `NODE_OPTIONS` would be persisted into managed services.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target: `src/daemon/service-env.test.ts`
- Covered scenarios: 97 tests total, including:
  - Default `--max-old-space-size=8192` when no NODE_OPTIONS present
  - Ambient NODE_OPTIONS with heap flag → dropped, default used
  - Ambient NODE_OPTIONS with other flags → dropped, default used
  - `--require` hostile flag → dropped
  - `--inspect` hostile flag → dropped
  - `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` override
  - `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` invalid → fallback to default
  - `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB=0` → disabled
  - `buildNodeServiceEnvironment` includes NODE_OPTIONS

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who set `NODE_OPTIONS` in their shell environment will no longer see those flags propagated to managed services. Only the `--max-old-space-size` heap flag is set. Users who need additional Node flags must use `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` or wait for an explicit allowlist mechanism.

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No
- [x] **Security hardening**: Hostile `NODE_OPTIONS` (`--require`, `--inspect`) from the host process are explicitly dropped from managed service environments

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Generated systemd gateway unit, heap override, node service unit, hostile flag injection, reinstall/upgrade
- Edge cases checked: Invalid `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB`, zero-disable, multi-flag ambient NODE_OPTIONS
- What you did NOT verify: `systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl start` on a real Linux host

## Best-fix Verdict

Only the OpenClaw-owned `--max-old-space-size` flag is generated. Ambient `NODE_OPTIONS` are never read. This is the narrowest fix that addresses the OOM issue without re-opening the startup preload boundary.

- **Best fix**: Yes
- **Refactor needed**: No
- **Alternative considered**: Preserving selected benign NODE_OPTIONS — rejected because there is no documented allowlist and maintainers should decide which flags are safe

## AI Assistance 🤖 (required for AI-assisted PRs)

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest-risk area**: Low-memory hosts (≤8 GB) where the 8192 MB default could cause swap pressure. The `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` override allows operators to tune this.
- **Mitigation**: Operators on low-memory hosts should set `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_HEAP_MB` to a lower value (e.g. 4096). The default was chosen to match the documented gateway memory requirements.
- **Compatibility impact**: Services that previously received ambient `NODE_OPTIONS` will now only receive `--max-old-space-size`. This is a security improvement.

---

Fixes #96203
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan
